name: AWS EKS Managed Node Group Platform
description: Creates a production-oriented Amazon EKS cluster with encrypted secrets, control plane logging, managed node groups, and core EKS add-ons.
architectureSummary: Builds an EKS control plane in existing private subnets, creates least-privilege IAM roles for the cluster and managed worker nodes, enables control plane logs, encrypts Kubernetes secrets with KMS, and installs the core AWS-managed EKS add-ons.
securityNotes: The cluster endpoint can be private-only. Kubernetes secrets are encrypted with a dedicated KMS key, worker nodes use managed policies scoped to EKS operation, and public API access CIDRs are explicit inputs.

versions.changelog: Initial EKS managed node group platform blueprint.

inputs.name: project_name
inputs.label: Project Name
inputs.description: Lowercase project name used for resource names.

inputs.name: subnet_ids
inputs.label: Private Subnet IDs
inputs.description: Comma-separated private subnet IDs for EKS and worker nodes.

inputs.name: endpoint_public_access
inputs.label: Public API Endpoint
inputs.description: Whether the Kubernetes API endpoint is public.

inputs.name: public_access_cidrs
inputs.label: Public API CIDRs
inputs.description: Comma-separated CIDRs allowed to reach the public API endpoint.

inputs.name: node_instance_types
inputs.label: Node Instance Types
inputs.description: Comma-separated EC2 instance types for the managed node group.
